title: Subagent Delegation UI — @general mentions in desktop composer
consumer_purpose: Allow desktop users to mention @general (and other subagents) in the composer prompt with visual distinction from regular agents
claim_atoms: Typing @ in the composer shows agent options including subagents (general, plan, etc.)
Subagents appear with distinct bot icon and optional description in the popover
Selecting a subagent creates an agent-type pill in the editor showing the subagent name
Subagent delegates are grouped between agents and recent files in the popover ordering
handleAtSelect routes delegate selection to addPart with type:agent for pipeline compatibility
No changes needed to ContentPart, AgentPart, createPill, parseFromDOM, pushAgent, or buildRequestParts
bun typecheck passes with zero errors in packages/app
revision_notes: Resolved 2 blocking critic findings: F1 (handleAtSelect) now includes delegate in the agent branch so delegates route to addPart with type:agent instead of crashing on missing option.path. F2 (groupBy) adds a separate delegate group with rank 0.5 so subagents appear between agents and recent files instead of falling through to the file group. Both fixes verified against current source code. Only 2 files changed (slash-popover.tsx and prompt-input.tsx).
content: # Plan: Subagent Delegation UI — @general mentions in desktop composer ## Root Causes **SG-001 (visual gap)**: The @mention popover already includes subagents in the agent list (filter: `mode !== "primary"`), but there is no visual distinction between regular agents and subagent delegation targets. Both render with the same `brain` icon. **SG-002 (type gap)**: The `AtOption` type supports `{type: "agent" | "file"}`. Subagents are shoehorned into `type: "agent"`. Adding `type: "delegate"` enables distinct rendering. ## Changeset (6 edits, 2 files) ### Fix A: Extend AtOption type (slash-popover.tsx:6-9) **Before**: ```ts export type AtOption = | { type: "agent"; name: string; display: string } | { type: "file"; path: string; display: string; recent?: boolean } ``` **After**: ```ts export type AtOption = | { type: "agent"; name: string; display: string } | { type: "delegate"; name: string; display: string; description?: string } | { type: "file"; path: string; display: string; recent?: boolean } ``` ### Fix B: Add delegate rendering in PromptPopover (slash-popover.tsx ~line 69) Insert a delegate rendering branch inside ``, between the agent branch (`if (item.type === "agent")`) and the file rendering fallthrough. **Insert after the agent render block**: ```tsx if (item.type === "delegate") { return ( ) } ``` > **Icon choice**: Uses `bot` icon instead of `brain` to visually distinguish delegates from regular agents. If `bot` is unavailable in the icon set, fall back to `user` or `corner-down-right`. ### Fix C: Map subagents to delegate type (prompt-input.tsx:584-588) **Before**: ```ts const agentList = createMemo(() => sync.data.agent .filter((agent) => !agent.hidden && agent.mode !== "primary") .map((agent): AtOption => ({ type: "agent", name: agent.name, display: agent.name })), ) ``` **After**: ```ts const agentList = createMemo(() => sync.data.agent .filter((agent) => !agent.hidden && agent.mode !== "primary") .map((agent): AtOption => agent.mode === "subagent" ? { type: "delegate", name: agent.name, display: agent.name, description: agent.description } : { type: "agent", name: agent.name, display: agent.name }), ) ``` ### Fix D: Add delegate key to atKey (prompt-input.tsx:600-603) **Before**: ```ts const atKey = (x: AtOption | undefined) => { if (!x) return "" return x.type === "agent" ? `agent:${x.name}` : `file:${x.path}` } ``` **After**: ```ts const atKey = (x: AtOption | undefined) => { if (!x) return "" if (x.type === "agent") return `agent:${x.name}` if (x.type === "delegate") return `delegate:${x.name}` return `file:${x.path}` } ``` ### Fix E: Handle delegate in handleAtSelect (prompt-input.tsx:591-598) **BLOCKING F1 FIX**: The critic found that selecting a delegate type option crashes because `option.type === "agent"` is false for delegates, falling to the else branch which accesses `option.path` (undefined on delegate type). **Repair**: Add `option.type === "delegate"` to the agent branch condition. Both agent and delegate have a `name` property. TypeScript narrows the else branch to `{ type: "file" }` exclusively. **Before**: ```ts const handleAtSelect = (option: AtOption | undefined) => { if (!option) return if (option.type === "agent") { addPart({ type: "agent", name: option.name, content: "@" + option.name, start: 0, end: 0 }) } else { addPart({ type: "file", path: option.path, content: "@" + option.path, start: 0, end: 0 }) } } ``` **After**: ```ts const handleAtSelect = (option: AtOption | undefined) => { if (!option) return if (option.type === "agent" || option.type === "delegate") { addPart({ type: "agent", name: option.name, content: "@" + option.name, start: 0, end: 0 }) } else { addPart({ type: "file", path: option.path, content: "@" + option.path, start: 0, end: 0 }) } } ``` > **Type safety**: With `option.type === "agent" || option.type === "delegate"`, TypeScript narrows the else branch to the remaining union member `{ type: "file"; path: string; ... }`. Both `path` access in else and `name` access in the if-branch are type-safe. The alternative explicit guard clause (`if (option.type === "delegate") return addPart({ type: "agent", ... })`) is identical in effect; the `||` form is chosen for conciseness. ### Fix F: Handle delegate in groupBy with its own group (prompt-input.tsx:622-631) **BLOCKING F2 FIX**: The critic found delegates with `type: "delegate"` don't match `item.type === "agent"` and don't have `item.recent`, so they fell through to `return "file"` — placing subagent options under the file section. **Repair**: Add a `"delegate"` group with rank 0.5 (between agents at 0 and recents at 1) so delegates appear in their own logical position. **Before**: ```ts groupBy: (item) => { if (item.type === "agent") return "agent" if (item.recent) return "recent" return "file" }, sortGroupsBy: (a, b) => { const rank = (category: string) => { if (category === "agent") return 0 if (category === "recent") return 1 return 2 } return rank(a.category) - rank(b.category) }, ``` **After**: ```ts groupBy: (item) => { if (item.type === "delegate") return "delegate" if (item.type === "agent") return "agent" if (item.recent) return "recent" return "file" }, sortGroupsBy: (a, b) => { const rank = (category: string) => { if (category === "agent") return 0 if (category === "delegate") return 0.5 if (category === "recent") return 1 return 2 } return rank(a.category) - rank(b.category) }, ``` > **Ordering rationale**: Agents (0) → Delegates (0.5) → Recent files (1) → Other files (2). Delegates are agent-like but visually distinct, so they sit between the two categories. The `groupBy` checks delegate BEFORE agent; this isn't required for correctness (both return different strings), but follows the linting pattern of checking more specific cases first. ## Impact Assessment ### Files changed (2 files only) 1. `packages/app/src/components/prompt-input/slash-popover.tsx` — Fix A (AtOption type) + Fix B (delegate render branch) 2. `packages/app/src/components/prompt-input.tsx` — Fix C (agentList map), Fix D (atKey), Fix E (handleAtSelect), Fix F (groupBy + sortGroupsBy) ### No changes to: - `context/prompt.tsx` — ContentPart union unchanged - `prompt-input/build-request-parts.ts` — already handles AgentPart - `context/local.tsx` — composer dropdown filter unchanged (correctly excludes subagents via `agent.mode !== "subagent"`) - `prompt-input/submit.tsx` — unchanged - `createPill` — unchanged (handles `type: "agent"` via same code path for both regular agents and delegates) - `parseFromDOM/pushAgent` — unchanged (reads `data-type="agent"` back) - `addPart` — unchanged (handles `type: "agent"` pill creation) - `utils/prompt.ts` — already handles agent parts generically - SDK agent type definitions — `sync.data.agent` already has `mode`, `name`, `description`, `hidden` properties ## Risks 1. **Icon name `bot` may not exist**: Verify in the Icon component's icon set before finalizing. Fallback: reuse `brain` with a different color class (e.g., `text-icon-info-subtle` instead of `text-icon-info-active`), or use `chat`, `message-square`, `corner-down-right`. 2. **atKey collision**: Not a practical risk — agent names and delegate names are unique across `sync.data.agent`. 3. **Backwards compat**: All existing @mentions create `type: "agent"` ContentPart — no regressions. Delegates also create `type: "agent"` parts for pipeline compatibility. 4. **Non-indexed description search**: `filterKeys` only specifies `["display"]`, so subagent descriptions won't be searchable. Consider adding `["display", "description"]` if UX feedback requests it. ## Validation 1. `bun typecheck` in `packages/app` — must pass with zero errors 2. Manual test: Type @ in composer, confirm subagents (general, plan) appear with `bot` icon and optional description 3. Manual test: Select @general, confirm pill created with @general text (same visual as agent pill) 4. Manual test: Submit prompt with @general, inspect request payload for correct agent part 5. Manual test: @file mentions still work 6. Manual test: @agent (non-subagent) mentions still work 7. Manual test: Popover ordering — agents first, delegates second, recent files third, other files last 8. Manual test: Keyboard selection of delegate via Enter works (reuses handleAtSelect path)
